slug: 颜色不存在_它是你大脑编出来的
title: 颜色不存在——它是你大脑编出来的

outline.concept: Light itself has no color—only wavelength. A 700nm wave isn't 'red' any more than a low bass note is 'dark.' Color is your brain's proprietary rendering of spectral data, not a property the external world carries.
outline.query: 彩虹 光 波长 spectrum 物理 prism 棱镜

outline.concept: Your retina samples the entire visible spectrum with just 3 cone types (S, M, L), each responding to a broad, overlapping band. Your brain triangulates a color from those 3 signals—it never directly reads wavelength. This is why missing one cone type (color blindness) collapses a whole dimension of color experience.
outline.query: 色盲 色弱 眼睛检查 辨色 cone 视锥 眼科

outline.concept: Identical wavelengths can produce different perceived colors depending on surrounding context—simultaneous contrast and color constancy. A gray patch on yellow looks blue-gray; on blue, yellow-gray. Your brain computes color relative to neighbors, not from absolute input, proving color is a comparative judgment, not a direct reading.
outline.query: 颜色错觉 视觉 trick 背景色 同一颜色 illusion 对比

outline.concept: Without a perceiving nervous system, the universe contains electromagnetic radiation but zero color. Color is a sensory user interface your brain overlays on reality to make wavelength differences behaviorally useful. You've never seen the world as it is—you've only ever seen your brain's dashboard.
outline.query: 闭上眼睛 黑暗 想象 看不见 如果 红色不存在 梦里颜色
